sentiment.summary: A devastating jobs miss and Middle East oil shock sent markets reeling — fear is dominating.

insights.text: Jobs collapse + Iran war premium = perfect storm. February payrolls fell 92K vs. +55K expected, unemployment rose to 4.4%, and oil spiked above $90 on U.S.-Israel strikes against Iran. VIX exploded to 29.49. Dow turned red for 2026. Cash is king this weekend.

BTC at $67,254 — $6K below your cost basis of $73,135. Fear & Greed at 18 (Extreme Fear). Hit $73K last week on ETF inflows, then reversed hard with macro. Jobs data may revive Fed cut hopes, but geopolitical risk keeps crypto sentiment crushed right now.

RKLB at $70.11 — solidly above your ~$62 cost basis. 83rd Electron mission launched March 6, record Q4 revenue $180M, backlog up 73% to $1.85B. Neutron delayed to Q4 2026 is a risk, but operational momentum is real. Holding strong in a very weak tape.

SATS at $109 — slightly below your $114.33 cost basis. SpaceX partnership is the new thesis: market now values SATS as a SpaceX proxy, not satellite TV. $16B impairment cleared the decks. Watch SpaceX valuation moves as the only real catalyst from here.

TE at $6.72 — right at your ~$6.73 cost basis, but catalysts are stacking. Section 45X tax credits confirmed under the One Big Beautiful Bill Act. G2_Austin fab breaking ground. Analyst targets up to $15.75. Solar manufacturing credits are real recurring revenue.

STX at $352 — down 22% from your $448.98 cost basis. Fundamentals strong: Q2 revenue $2.83B, analyst targets at $475, AI storage supercycle intact. Deep underwater in a macro selloff. HAMR tech and hyperscaler demand remain the long-term thesis — patience required.

TSAT at $30.84 — below your $33.33 cost basis. Lightspeed LEO construction starting (antennas spring 2026) and military comms partnerships developing. Pre-revenue LEO bet in a market preferring safety. High execution risk — hold only for the LEO network thesis.

BBAI at $4.24 — down 53% from your $9.02 cost basis. Q4 revenue cratered 37.5% to $27.3M. Upside: 90% debt reduction, $462M cash, 2026 guidance $135-165M on Middle East expansion. Stockholder meeting March 18. Turnaround story — but execution must prove out.

NBIS at $89 — down 32% from your $131.12 cost basis. Q4 revenue $227.7M (+547% YoY), Missouri AI campus approved, Microsoft/Meta deals inked. The gap between fundamentals and price is striking. AI infrastructure secular tailwind is intact — patience required here.

OKLO at $61 — down 51% from your $125 cost basis. Analysts warn of $50 if $60 support breaks. DOE facility win and $1.68B Tennessee fuel recycling plant are real milestones. Nuclear plays need patience — revenue years away. Watch the $60 level closely this week.

NVTS at $8.90 — below your $10.00 cost basis. Morgan Stanley Sell rating, Jefferies cut target to $9. APEC conference March 22-26 could provide a catalyst. GaN/SiC for AI data centers is the thesis, but execution has disappointed. Earnings May 7 is the next test.
